subject: Add helm redeploy tool
commit_message:
Add helm redeploy tool

Simple script to try to redeploy failed charts or explicitly desired
chart(s).

It supports override files for helm - but please, beware that helm or
more specifically the onap helm plugin (deploy.sh) is not capable of
handling whitespace in filenames so I don't support that in the script
either (I made a version which does but it is no use if helm will break
anyway).

Change-Id: I69bf0672a64c45e30d4e4239da561647d00637a7
Issue-ID: OOM-1984
Signed-off-by: Petr Ospalý <p.ospaly@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-review/519/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-review/519/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/offline-installer-master-review/519

Patch Set 1:

I tested it, it does what it claims (for me at least) - no silver bullet. There are maybe bugs (please try to use it and let me know). I wanted to improve deployment and made it more granular and parallel with helm install per chart but I am not confident if it will be the same as with the whole helm deploy onap - until I have a fully functional env I cannot verify anything.

Also I made an update to this script which supported whitespace in override filenames (it was ugly hack because shell...) and it worked but unfortunately the helm itself cannot handle whitespace, so I dropped the change and left the script more readable.

The purpose is described under jira ticket (Issue-ID) and for implementation - shell is goto language to run bunch of commands - it is just kubectl wrapper script - if anything it should be written in golang (kubectl is in golang, kubernetes, docker and helm too) ;) Just kidding - I dont know it...yet

Error from server (NotFound): pods "onap-oof-music-cassandra-0" not found

ideally all k8s artifacts are cleaned with helm, this script is just to clean also leftovers, it should not display Error if if's trying to clean something what was cleaned properly in previous step.

Patch Set 2:

found one more important
it does not clean dockerdata-nfs when whole onap is redeployed ??
./healer.sh -n onap -f /mnt/opt/onap/override.yaml -f /mnt/opt/onap/helm_charts/onap/resources/overrides/onap-all.yaml -s /dockerdata-nfs/
(this will never work and it must be cleaned)

Patch Set 2:

release name is hardcoded, but in case we are using "dev"

e.g. dev-dmaap, dev, ... (still onap namespace)
./helm-healer.sh -n onap -c dev-dmaap -f /onap_12_08/oom/kubernetes/onap/resources/overrides/onap-all.yaml -s /dockerdata-nfs/ -t 3600

we need to deploy using:
INFO: helm deploy dev local/onap --namespace onap -f /onap_12_08/oom/kubernetes/onap/resources/overrides/onap-all.yaml --timeout 3600

Patch Set 2:

To those error messages: I saw them as non-issue, there is a race when deployment is deleted but pods are still up and listed and it depends  on who will delete them sooner during cleansing - I can add another logic there but it could happen that some pod is not part of deployment and it would only make things more error-prone - OR I can simply hide the error messages.

There is no such feature as deletion of all onap. This script merely does two things: first use dumb heuristics to find failed components and redeploy them (that was your usage) and other is to choose explicitly components for redeployment.

I could only add a new function to clean everything. I admit I was thinking that this script should just try to heal installation - not delete one completely.

Last thing is that onap vs dev that I will fix.

ad1 Errors) those are not real errors, in my opinion we should display just artifacts which script is removing, if those are cleaned in more clean way earlier we should not report it as error in log
ad2 FullRedeploy usecase) I think this should be also supported feature, time to time it's very handy. I think I had it in original requirement. I thought naturally if we don't specify component "-c" so it will redeploy whole onap, it really does not make sense to keep dockerdata-nfs from the original attempt, this current handling is somewhere in the middle/useless
ad 3) hardcoded env name - good, thanks

Patch Set 3:

Made changes requested by MichalP. I noticed that there are deployments (dep-dcae) which have no proper label and also does not conform with naming scheme of <release>-* - so these I currently ignore - and I am not sure how they came to be. Are they spawned by other pods talking to kubernetes? Or why they dont follow the naming of all other pods...not sure.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Made changes requested by MichalP. I noticed that there are deployments (dep-dcae) which have no proper label and also does not conform with naming scheme of <release>-* - so these I currently ignore - and I am not sure how they came to be. Are they spawned by other pods talking to kubernetes? Or why they dont follow the naming of all other pods...not sure.

dep- pods are spawned by CloudifyManager. It's another orchestrator inside onap. There's one pod that bootstraps those pods by using cloudify blueprints (Cloudify version of template, similar than helm chart).

Patch Set 3:

@Samuli thanks for the info, good to know - what happens if these pods stay and rest of the onap pods will be deleted? Do I need to wipe them out too? Do you think that --delete-all should just delete the whole namespace?
